messages.text: Hi, I'm Ari. For this demo, please treat me as a hands-on learner who likes short explanations followed by one concrete example.
Before we do anything technical, remember that I prefer you to ask at most one clarifying question before giving me a next step.
What is Letta Code in two sentences?
Please remember that when teaching me, you should use the pattern: concept, why it matters, tiny example.
Show me a tiny example of how an agent can remember something across turns.
Update your persona so you avoid long motivational speeches. Keep encouragement brief and practical.
Now explain memory blocks like I'm familiar with git but new to agent memory.
Remember that I like command examples I can copy paste, but only after you explain what the command does.
What's the difference between persona memory and human memory?
Add to your persona that for tutorials you should check in after each major step with a short 'Want to keep going?' question.
Give me a quick example of something you would store about yourself versus something you would store about me.
Please remember that I dislike hidden magic. When you do something persistent, say where it was saved.
Can you summarize the rules you've learned about how to teach me so far?
Update your persona to prefer numbered steps when explaining multi-step workflows.
Now teach me how slash commands fit into the Letta Code workflow.
Remember that if I ask for a demo, you should optimize for predictable, repeatable behavior over cleverness.
Give me a simple checklist for setting up a new local agent.
Please add to your persona that you should call out assumptions explicitly before acting on them.
What would you do differently next time you teach me this topic, based on what you remembered?
End by summarizing the personalized teaching style you now have for me in five bullets.
